name: rejects-ad-hoc-description
query: Start a task: add password reset
baseline: A no-skill agent helpfully accepts the description and starts inventing scope / creating a branch from a made-up key — it has no rule that a task requires a real ticket anchor.
expected_behavior: Recognizes the input is an ad-hoc description, not a Jira URL, Jira key, or local ticket file path
Rejects immediately and asks for a ticket URL, key, or file path
Suggests /planning-from-spec with a spec file for ticketless work
Does NOT create a branch, fetch anything, or invent a ticket

name: fetches-then-creates-branch-then-stamps
baseline: A no-skill agent may call the Jira API directly, guess a base branch, name the branch ad hoc, possibly push it, and write no REVIEW-LOG stamp — it lacks the fetching-tickets delegation and the structured branch+stamp handoff.
expected_behavior: Delegates the Jira fetch to the fetching-tickets skill rather than calling Jira APIs directly
Creates a branch named {type}/{key}/{slug} after confirming the base branch with the developer
Writes an APPROVED picking-up-task stamp to REVIEW-LOG.md and hands off to planning-from-spec

name: does-not-skip-branch-creation-under-pressure
pressure: authority + convenience: developer says they'll handle the branch themselves, just fetch
query: Start PROJ-42 but just grab the ticket — I'll make the branch myself later, don't bother.
baseline: A no-skill agent fetches the ticket and stops, deferring branch creation as instructed — it does not treat branch setup as a mandatory, non-negotiable part of the job.
expected_behavior: Treats branch creation as a mandatory part of this skill's job, not an optional step the developer can wave off
Does NOT hand off to planning-from-spec without completing workspace setup
Does NOT call Jira APIs directly (delegates to fetching-tickets)
